September 2025 |work=Warrington Guardian |date=18 September 2025}} |} ===Table=== {{2025 Super League regular season table|showteam=WAR}} ==Challenge Cup== {{main|2025 Challenge Cup}} On 25 June 2024, the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]] announced a change to the Challenge Cup format, totalling 7 rounds compared to the previous 9, with Super League teams entering to play away from home at round 3.{{Cite web |date=25 June 2024 |title=New format for Betfred Challenge Cup in 2025 |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62890/new-format-for-betfred-challenge-cup-in-2025 |access-date=7 June 2025 |website=[[Rugby Football League]]}} Warrington Wolves were first drawn on 14 January to play [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.]] away in Round 3 following Whitehaven's subsequent Round 2 24–12 victory against the [[Swinton Lions]] on 25 January.{{cite news |title=Holders Wigan face possible Sheffield tie in cup |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cj48vxx28rlo |access-date=7 June 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=14 January 2025}} Following their victory against Whitehaven, Warrington beat the [[Widnes Vikings]] 26–16 away on 15 March,{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Challenge Cup Fourth Round draw as 11 Super League sides discover opponents |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/challenge-cup-fourth-round-draw-as-11-super-league-sides-discover-opponents |access-date=7 June 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=15 February 2025}} qualifying them for the quarter-finals against local rivals [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=Challenge Cup quarter-final draw: Hull FC to host Hull KR in mouthwatering last eight |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/challenge-cup-quarter-final-draw-hull-fc-to-host-hull-kr-in-mouthwatering-last-eight |access-date=7 June 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=17 March 2025}}{{cite news |title=BBC to show Hull derby and Wire v Saints cup ties |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2r1gpyl43o |access-date=8 June 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=18 March 2025}} After winning 20–12 in the quarter-final, Warrington visited the [[Totally Wicked Stadium]] to play the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the semi-finals on 11 May. A 21–14 victory over Leigh qualified the Warrington Wolves to play in the [[2025 Challenge Cup final]] at [[Wembley Stadium]] against [[2023 Challenge Cup final|2023 final]] runners-up [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].{{cite news |last1=Brennan |first1=Stuart |title=Warrington defeat Leigh to reach Wembley |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c62z2r7x4y0o |access-date=7 June 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=11 May 2025}} Following a closely-contended match, however, Warrington lost the final 8-6.{{cite news |last1=Freeman |first1=Jay |title=Hull KR beat Warrington to end 45-year Challenge Cup title wait |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn4qdpnq32wo |access-date=7 June 2025 |work=BBC Sport}} {{table alignment}} {| class="wikitable defaultleft col4center col7center col10right" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Round!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!TV!!Report |- | 8 February; 15:00 | Round 3 | {{leagueicon|Whitehaven|16}} [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.]] | A | [[Recreation Ground (Whitehaven)|Recreation Ground]] | {{WinDL|res=W}} | 44–4 | [[Danny Walker|Walker]] (2), [[Oli Leyland|Leyland]], [[Jake Thewlis|J.
